title: US and Iran trade strikes, threatening fragile truce
subhead: Iran's central military command accused the US of violating the ceasefire with attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, saying they struck US forces in retaliation. The US, however, says Iran attacked three American destroyers first, which Trump later dismissed as a 'trifle,' and said he considered the ceasefire still active.

Welsh nationalists poised for historic election breakthrough
subhead: For the first time in its history, the pro-independence Welsh party Plaid Cymru could top the Welsh Parliament in Thursday's election. The surge reflects not just disillusionment with rivals but a deeper frustration at years of being sidelined by London.

title: Trump and Brazil's Lula hail positive talks after rocky relations
subhead: The three-hour meeting came as a boost for Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva, who is seeking a fourth, non-consecutive term in office – and is facing a tight election in October against Flavio Bolsonaro, the son of former far-right president Jair Bolsonaro, a close Trump ally.

title: After PSG qualifies for the Champions League final, fans face travel headaches getting to Budapest
subhead: Paris Saint-Germain supporters hoping to attend the match against Arsenal on May 30 in the Hungarian capital must face high prices and the club's very selective ticket allocation process.

title: Russia, Israel, United States: 'Pariah' pavilions spark controversy at Venice Biennale
subhead: The major international art event, which this year reopened its doors to Russia, is facing widespread protests. On the ground, artists are divided.

subhead: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

title: On Algeria, Macron has a goal for his last year in office: Reconciliation
subhead: The French president, who began his first term aiming to ease historical tensions, now faces the most severe crisis in bilateral relations since Algeria's independence in 1962.
